creator: rodrigo@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Brad D from comment #8)

Thanks for the new patch :)

I found multiple issues in your port, let's try to address them one by one

I don't like the way upstream manages the versioning by having a "current"
archive the replace without keeping the history. This gonna for sure break
you build at every upgrade. Instead you can use sourceforge ability to
generate git snapshots of the codes, like this:

PORTNAME=	tinyfiledialogs
PORTVERSION=	3.17.5
DISTNAME=	${PORTNAME}-code-01da124032ca3c048fc7bf8056743c16d1d42fcf
CATEGORIES=	x11
MASTER_SITES=	https://sourceforge.net/code-snapshots/git/t/ti/tinyfiledialogs/code.git/

The port name should be tinyfiledialogs (x11/tinyfiledialogs)

No PORTREVISION, this is a new port
See https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/#makefile-portrevision

I suggest you to look at the port linters (portlint, porclipy and portfmt)
to cleanup your port. Currently portlint reports 2 fatal errors and 11 warnings
https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/#porting-portlint
https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/#testing-portclippy

Also having a working poudriere can help to test your port
https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/#porting-testing

It's common w<hen people submit an update for ports to request a poudriere status
to ensure people did the minimal tests on their ports before submitting them.
If you plan to do regular contributions having it will improve your PRs :)

Feel free to ask any questions, or submit new versions.
You can also submit your changes via our code review system (reviews.freebsd.org)
or you feel more comfortable with github, cloning the port tree and
submit your changes as a push request.
